name: OSU Aqualung and CMS (R2) - with NA comments

origin.filename: OSU Aqualung and CMS (R2) - with NA comments.pdf

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Ohio State University (OSU) has developed a flat-sheet, amine-based facilitated transport membrane (FTM) for carbon capture.  The membrane exhibits high CO 2 permeance of up to 4,000 gas permeation units (GPU) and a CO 2 /N 2 selectivity greater than 140.  OSU has been able to manufacture the membranes at scale (10's of m 2 ) and has used them in spiral wound housings containing 35m 2 of membrane.
texts.text: Ohio State University (OSU) has developed a flat-sheet, amine-based facilitated transport membrane (FTM) for carbon capture.  The membrane exhibits high CO 2 permeance of up to 4,000 gas permeation units (GPU) and a CO 2 /N 2 selectivity greater than 140.  OSU has been able to manufacture the membranes at scale (10's of m 2 ) and has used them in spiral wound housings containing 35m 2 of membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The membrane has been tested at a scale (around 60 tonnes per annum CO 2 captured) in a project conducted by OSU between July 2019 through March 2023 at the US National Carbon Capture Centre.
texts.text: The membrane has been tested at a scale (around 60 tonnes per annum CO 2 captured) in a project conducted by OSU between July 2019 through March 2023 at the US National Carbon Capture Centre.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The information in this paper is substantially taken from the final report of the aforementioned project.
texts.text: The information in this paper is substantially taken from the final report of the aforementioned project.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OSU's u00A0 membrane consists of a dense thin (170 nm) selective polymer layer coated onto a polymer substrate layer formed onto a nonwoven fabric backing.  The membrane exhibits a high CO 2 /N 2 selectivity (>140), characteristic of facilitated transport membranes, and also a good permeance (4,000 GPU), characteristic of flat sheet membranes.
texts.text: OSU's u00A0 membrane consists of a dense thin (170 nm) selective polymer layer coated onto a polymer substrate layer formed onto a nonwoven fabric backing.  The membrane exhibits a high CO 2 /N 2 selectivity (>140), characteristic of facilitated transport membranes, and also a good permeance (4,000 GPU), characteristic of flat sheet membranes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The permeance is driven by temperature with the quoted 4,000 GPU being achieved at 77˚C.  The permeance falls off rapidly with temperature, being less than 2,500 GPU at 67˚C.
texts.text: The permeance is driven by temperature with the quoted 4,000 GPU being achieved at 77˚C.  The permeance falls off rapidly with temperature, being less than 2,500 GPU at 67˚C.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In facilitated transport membranes, high GPU (SI equivalent units mol/m 2 .s.Pa) results can be obtained at low pressures, however, the actual flow across the membrane is determined by the transmission of CO 2 from carrier site to carrier site and by the availability of carrier sites.  Carrier saturation can occur when there are no free carrier molecules available to bond with carbon dioxide molecules.  This means that the actual flow of CO 2 across the membrane can reach a maximum irrespective of the differential pressure across the membrane (i.e. the apparent GPU will decrease with increasing pressure).  Thus, while a high headline GPU suggests a low membrane area, in reality the permeance will be much lower and large membrane areas will be required.
texts.text: In facilitated transport membranes, high GPU (SI equivalent units mol/m 2 .s.Pa) results can be obtained at low pressures, however, the actual flow across the membrane is determined by the transmission of CO 2 from carrier site to carrier site and by the availability of carrier sites.  Carrier saturation can occur when there are no free carrier molecules available to bond with carbon dioxide molecules.  This means that the actual flow of CO 2 across the membrane can reach a maximum irrespective of the differential pressure across the membrane (i.e. the apparent GPU will decrease with increasing pressure).  Thus, while a high headline GPU suggests a low membrane area, in reality the permeance will be much lower and large membrane areas will be required.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The membrane does not have a protective layer, meaning the active layer is directly exposed to the feed gas and any contaminants present.  The absence of a protective layer also means that any manufacturing defects (i.e. gaps) in the selective layer, which will occur, will lead to localised flow through the defect reducing the apparent selectivity of the membrane.
texts.text: The membrane does not have a protective layer, meaning the active layer is directly exposed to the feed gas and any contaminants present.  The absence of a protective layer also means that any manufacturing defects (i.e. gaps) in the selective layer, which will occur, will lead to localised flow through the defect reducing the apparent selectivity of the membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Facilitated transport membranes are susceptible to damage from contaminants, which can react with and deactivate the selective layer.  OSU's process design includes pre-treatment to remove SO 2 (flue gas desulphurisation followed by caustic scrubbing) to extremely low levels.  The pre-treatment also includes humidification of the feed stream, which is required by the facilitated transport mechanism.
texts.text: Facilitated transport membranes are susceptible to damage from contaminants, which can react with and deactivate the selective layer.  OSU's process design includes pre-treatment to remove SO 2 (flue gas desulphurisation followed by caustic scrubbing) to extremely low levels.  The pre-treatment also includes humidification of the feed stream, which is required by the facilitated transport mechanism.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OSU is utilising the membrane in spiral wound housings, which they say are commercial.  The housings have a high packing density, but each housing will only contain around 60m 2 of membrane, meaning that thousands of housings will be require for a typical large carbon capture plant.
texts.text: OSU is utilising the membrane in spiral wound housings, which they say are commercial.  The housings have a high packing density, but each housing will only contain around 60m 2 of membrane, meaning that thousands of housings will be require for a typical large carbon capture plant.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The housing has an extremely high pressure drop of 1.5 psi/m on each of the feed and permeate sides - a total pressure drop of around 3 psi/housing - or in terms of membrane area 1 psi/20m 2 (where housings are used in series).  The pressure drop will give rise to a cooling of the gas across each housing.  Across the entire membrane system, this cooling effect will be significant.  Additionally, since the feed/retentate gas is close to 80˚C, environmental cooling will occur.  It would be impractical to thermally insulate each module and interconnecting pipework to prevent this.
texts.text: The housing has an extremely high pressure drop of 1.5 psi/m on each of the feed and permeate sides - a total pressure drop of around 3 psi/housing - or in terms of membrane area 1 psi/20m 2 (where housings are used in series).  The pressure drop will give rise to a cooling of the gas across each housing.  Across the entire membrane system, this cooling effect will be significant.  Additionally, since the feed/retentate gas is close to 80˚C, environmental cooling will occur.  It would be impractical to thermally insulate each module and interconnecting pipework to prevent this.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The effect of this cooling and expansion of the gas can be expected to have several consequences which would not have been apparent in the small-scale pilot:
texts.text: The effect of this cooling and expansion of the gas can be expected to have several consequences which would not have been apparent in the small-scale pilot:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) The gas is already water saturated at inlet conditions.  The cooling will take the gas below its dew point and water will start to condense in the membrane system.  This will give rise to the formation of weak acids, if any SO 2 remains, which will damage the membranes.
texts.text: The gas is already water saturated at inlet conditions.  The cooling will take the gas below its dew point and water will start to condense in the membrane system.  This will give rise to the formation of weak acids, if any SO 2 remains, which will damage the membranes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) As the gas expands the flow rate will increase in the downstream membrane modules.  The effect of this will be to alter the membrane performance and also give rise to higher pressure drops in downstream modules, potentially exacerbating the effects.
texts.text: As the gas expands the flow rate will increase in the downstream membrane modules.  The effect of this will be to alter the membrane performance and also give rise to higher pressure drops in downstream modules, potentially exacerbating the effects.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) The permeance of downstream modules will fall with the lower temperature.
texts.text: The permeance of downstream modules will fall with the lower temperature.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The effect of this can be overcome using additional heating and intermediate compression throughout the membrane system, however, this will add complexity and cost.  The obvious solution would be to change the format of the membrane module.
texts.text: The effect of this can be overcome using additional heating and intermediate compression throughout the membrane system, however, this will add complexity and cost.  The obvious solution would be to change the format of the membrane module.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The high headline selectivity and permeance figures achieved in the laboratory and in the small pilot test require very specific humidity and temperature operating conditions.  Away from the ideal conditions the performance of the membrane drops away very quickly, or the membrane will not work at all.
texts.text: The high headline selectivity and permeance figures achieved in the laboratory and in the small pilot test require very specific humidity and temperature operating conditions.  Away from the ideal conditions the performance of the membrane drops away very quickly, or the membrane will not work at all.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The ideal conditions will be exceptionally difficult to achieve across a large membrane area and will require multiple intermediate, energy-intensive gas conditioning stages (heating and repressurisation) throughout the membrane area.  CAPEX is likely to be very expensive and energy use far more intensive than with CPT's PolyActive™ membrane.
texts.text: The ideal conditions will be exceptionally difficult to achieve across a large membrane area and will require multiple intermediate, energy-intensive gas conditioning stages (heating and repressurisation) throughout the membrane area.  CAPEX is likely to be very expensive and energy use far more intensive than with CPT's PolyActive™ membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The susceptibility of the membrane to contaminants and damage from condensed water is also a real concern.
texts.text: The susceptibility of the membrane to contaminants and damage from condensed water is also a real concern.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The performance achieve appears to be similar, if not worse, than CPT's PolyActive™ based process.
texts.text: The performance achieve appears to be similar, if not worse, than CPT's PolyActive™ based process.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Both membranes are flat-sheet thin film composite membranes.  At first sight OSU's u00A0 facilitated transport membrane would seem to have superior properties to PolyActive™, however, realising these characteristics in large-scale real-world applications is likely to be extremely challenging and at a significantly increased CAPEX over CPT's process.
texts.text: Both membranes are flat-sheet thin film composite membranes.  At first sight OSU's u00A0 facilitated transport membrane would seem to have superior properties to PolyActive™, however, realising these characteristics in large-scale real-world applications is likely to be extremely challenging and at a significantly increased CAPEX over CPT's process.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Despite the headline selectivity and permeance figures the actual performance of OSU's membrane 'in the field' appears to be worse than CPT's PolyActive™ membrane.  In the project at the National Carbon Capture Centre, with a feed CO 2 concentration of 13%, OSU achieved a purity of 37% CO 2 downstream of the first-stage membrane.  In similar conditions, CPT achieves a purity of 50-60% downstream of the first-stage membrane.  The low concentration achieved by OSU's membrane is potentially due to the sweep gas use to increase the driving force across the membrane.  This will have the impact of diluting this stream.  CPT is also able to achieve the 95% purity from a 2-stage membrane process.
texts.text: Despite the headline selectivity and permeance figures the actual performance of OSU's membrane 'in the field' appears to be worse than CPT's PolyActive™ membrane.  In the project at the National Carbon Capture Centre, with a feed CO 2 concentration of 13%, OSU achieved a purity of 37% CO 2 downstream of the first-stage membrane.  In similar conditions, CPT achieves a purity of 50-60% downstream of the first-stage membrane.  The low concentration achieved by OSU's membrane is potentially due to the sweep gas use to increase the driving force across the membrane.  This will have the impact of diluting this stream.  CPT is also able to achieve the 95% purity from a 2-stage membrane process.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPT's PolyActive™ membrane has a protective layer, which serves to protect the selective layer and to fill any manufacturing gaps in the very thin protective layer.  OSU's membrane has no such protective layer which means the membrane will be more easily damaged during operation and susceptible to manufacturing defects.
texts.text: CPT's PolyActive™ membrane has a protective layer, which serves to protect the selective layer and to fill any manufacturing gaps in the very thin protective layer.  OSU's membrane has no such protective layer which means the membrane will be more easily damaged during operation and susceptible to manufacturing defects.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OSU has just completed a pilot project at a scale similar to the pilot projects carried out by Helmholtz/CPT in 2014, 2019 and 2022.  Further scale-up to 10,000 tpa is expected to be achieved by CPT in 2024 whilst OSU is predicting that they will not be in this position until 2026.
texts.text: OSU has just completed a pilot project at a scale similar to the pilot projects carried out by Helmholtz/CPT in 2014, 2019 and 2022.  Further scale-up to 10,000 tpa is expected to be achieved by CPT in 2024 whilst OSU is predicting that they will not be in this position until 2026.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OSU is more likely to encounter problems in further scaling due to the specific operating requirements of their facilitated transport membrane.  Contaminants, particularly SO 2 , need to be removed from the feed gas to very low levels.  Additionally, the feed gas needs to be water saturated for the facilitated transport mechanism to work.  Meeting this requirement whilst avoiding water condensing in the membrane housings will be extremely difficult to achieve across the large membrane areas required for large-scale carbon capture.  CPT's PolyActive™ membrane is tolerant of contaminants and can work over a wide range of operating conditions.  Avoiding water condensation is, therefore, relatively straight forward and major scaling problems in scaling are not anticipated.
texts.text: OSU is more likely to encounter problems in further scaling due to the specific operating requirements of their facilitated transport membrane.  Contaminants, particularly SO 2 , need to be removed from the feed gas to very low levels.  Additionally, the feed gas needs to be water saturated for the facilitated transport mechanism to work.  Meeting this requirement whilst avoiding water condensing in the membrane housings will be extremely difficult to achieve across the large membrane areas required for large-scale carbon capture.  CPT's PolyActive™ membrane is tolerant of contaminants and can work over a wide range of operating conditions.  Avoiding water condensation is, therefore, relatively straight forward and major scaling problems in scaling are not anticipated.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OSU is proposing the use of spiral wound housings which have a very high pressure loss (in the region of 1 psi/20m 2 ).  This pressure drop equates to energy loss which will have a very significant impact on the energy (kWh/tonne of CO 2 captured) required for capture.  CPT's housing has been designed to have extremely low pressure losses (in the region of 1 psi/10,000m 2 ).
texts.text: OSU is proposing the use of spiral wound housings which have a very high pressure loss (in the region of 1 psi/20m 2 ).  This pressure drop equates to energy loss which will have a very significant impact on the energy (kWh/tonne of CO 2 captured) required for capture.  CPT's housing has been designed to have extremely low pressure losses (in the region of 1 psi/10,000m 2 ).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Large temperature losses can be expected across OSU's membrane area in part due to the large pressure loss and in part due to environmental losses.  This temperature loss will exacerbate the challenge of maintaining water saturation throughout the membrane whilst avoiding condensation. Lower temperatures in downstream membrane modules will have a significant impact on membrane permeance, which is markedly reduced at lower temperatures.  CPT's housing is inherently insulated and will not suffer the same temperature impact from either pressure or environmental losses.
texts.text: Large temperature losses can be expected across OSU's membrane area in part due to the large pressure loss and in part due to environmental losses.  This temperature loss will exacerbate the challenge of maintaining water saturation throughout the membrane whilst avoiding condensation. Lower temperatures in downstream membrane modules will have a significant impact on membrane permeance, which is markedly reduced at lower temperatures.  CPT's housing is inherently insulated and will not suffer the same temperature impact from either pressure or environmental losses.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Consequently, the design of plants around OSU's membrane can be expected to be significantly more challenging with operating conditions varying significantly across the membrane area.  Plants will, therefore, be complex and susceptible to operational problems possibly resulting in damage to the membranes and more frequent change-out.  Achieving high levels of reliability can be expected to be challenging.  Plants designed around PolyActive™ are, in comparison, simple.  Operational problems are less likely to be encountered and high reliability and membrane life can be expected.
texts.text: Consequently, the design of plants around OSU's membrane can be expected to be significantly more challenging with operating conditions varying significantly across the membrane area.  Plants will, therefore, be complex and susceptible to operational problems possibly resulting in damage to the membranes and more frequent change-out.  Achieving high levels of reliability can be expected to be challenging.  Plants designed around PolyActive™ are, in comparison, simple.  Operational problems are less likely to be encountered and high reliability and membrane life can be expected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Despite the high permeance quoted, for the reasons given above, actual membrane areas are likely to be much larger using OSU's process than using CPT's process.  In addition, CPT's process can be operated at a higher pressure.
texts.text: Despite the high permeance quoted, for the reasons given above, actual membrane areas are likely to be much larger using OSU's process than using CPT's process.  In addition, CPT's process can be operated at a higher pressure.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It is believed that the process complexity required for FTM will make the CAPEX of plants using OSU's membrane significantly more expensive than CPT's plants.  The extremely high-pressure loss in the OSU system will make the system much more energy intensive than CPT's plant.  The resulting cost of capture can be expected to be significantly higher.
texts.text: It is believed that the process complexity required for FTM will make the CAPEX of plants using OSU's membrane significantly more expensive than CPT's plants.  The extremely high-pressure loss in the OSU system will make the system much more energy intensive than CPT's plant.  The resulting cost of capture can be expected to be significantly higher.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In 2023 OSU completed an R&D project using actual flue gas at the US National Carbon Capture Center.  The project ran from July 2019 through March 2023 and was funded substantially between the US DOE ($2,999,988) and OSU ($740,000).
texts.text: In 2023 OSU completed an R&D project using actual flue gas at the US National Carbon Capture Center.  The project ran from July 2019 through March 2023 and was funded substantially between the US DOE ($2,999,988) and OSU ($740,000).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The aim of the project was to develop a cost-effective design and fabrication process for a novel transformational membrane and its membrane modules that capture CO 2 from flue gas with 60 - 90% CO 2 recovery and at least 95% CO 2 purity.
texts.text: The aim of the project was to develop a cost-effective design and fabrication process for a novel transformational membrane and its membrane modules that capture CO 2 from flue gas with 60 - 90% CO 2 recovery and at least 95% CO 2 purity.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · To achieve 60 to 90% capture of CO 2 with greater than 95% CO 2 purity ready for compression to 152 bar (2,200 psi) for storage or enhanced oil recovery and for less than $30/tonne CO 2 captured
texts.text: To achieve 60 to 90% capture of CO 2 with greater than 95% CO 2 purity ready for compression to 152 bar (2,200 psi) for storage or enhanced oil recovery and for less than $30/tonne CO 2 captured

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Synthesize an improved polymer support with a CO 2 permeance greater than 23,000 gas permeation units (GPU)
texts.text: Synthesize an improved polymer support with a CO 2 permeance greater than 23,000 gas permeation units (GPU)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Develop a polymeric composite membrane with CO 2 permeance greater than 3,300 GPU and CO 2 /N 2 selectivity more than 140 at 77°C
texts.text: Develop a polymeric composite membrane with CO 2 permeance greater than 3,300 GPU and CO 2 /N 2 selectivity more than 140 at 77°C

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Design and construct an integrated bench-scale testing system to be tested at OSU and NCCC
texts.text: Design and construct an integrated bench-scale testing system to be tested at OSU and NCCC

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The process took flue gas with a 13% CO 2 concentration from a coal-fired boiler.  A 2-stage membrane process was used to remove up to 90% of the CO 2 from the flue gas and export the CO 2 rich stream with a purity of >95%.
texts.text: The process took flue gas with a 13% CO 2 concentration from a coal-fired boiler.  A 2-stage membrane process was used to remove up to 90% of the CO 2 from the flue gas and export the CO 2 rich stream with a purity of >95%.

texts.text: Process Concept for Two-Stage Membrane System (at start of project in 2019)

texts.text: FGD = flue gas desulfurization; SCS = SO 2 caustic scrubber; BL = blower; MB = membrane; EX = turbo expander; HX = heat exchanger; KO = knockout; VAC = vacuum pump; MSC = multi-stage compressor.

texts.orig: It can be seen from the diagram below that SO 2 removal and humidification of the feed is required upstream of the membrane modules.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The project appears to have been successfully completed with the required selectivity and permeance being achieved.  Flow rates for the actual test were not given but the pre-laboratory testing was carried out at a rate of 1.33 tonnes/day, which is approximately 60 tonnes per annum of CO 2 captured.
texts.text: The project appears to have been successfully completed with the required selectivity and permeance being achieved.  Flow rates for the actual test were not given but the pre-laboratory testing was carried out at a rate of 1.33 tonnes/day, which is approximately 60 tonnes per annum of CO 2 captured.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OSU has developed an amine-based flat-sheet facilitated transport membrane (FTM). Their u00A0 membrane consists of a dense thin (170 nm) selective polymer layer coated onto a polymer substrate layer formed onto a nonwoven fabric backing.
texts.text: OSU has developed an amine-based flat-sheet facilitated transport membrane (FTM). Their u00A0 membrane consists of a dense thin (170 nm) selective polymer layer coated onto a polymer substrate layer formed onto a nonwoven fabric backing.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The membrane's facilitated transport mechanism appears to feature both fixed and mobile carrier sites as well as physical diffusion through the polymer (solution diffusion).
texts.text: The membrane's facilitated transport mechanism appears to feature both fixed and mobile carrier sites as well as physical diffusion through the polymer (solution diffusion).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Efficient and Scalable Membrane for Low Cost
texts.text: Efficient and Scalable Membrane for Low Cost

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It should be noted that the membrane has no protective layer so any damage to the membrane will directly affect the selective layer.
texts.text: It should be noted that the membrane has no protective layer so any damage to the membrane will directly affect the selective layer.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OSU has demonstrated the manufacture of their membrane at scale using a continuous roll-to-roll manufacturing process.  Membranes have been manufactured using a vapor-induced phase separation process at a width of 21-inch and a 1,300 ft in length per casting run.
texts.text: OSU has demonstrated the manufacture of their membrane at scale using a continuous roll-to-roll manufacturing process.  Membranes have been manufactured using a vapor-induced phase separation process at a width of 21-inch and a 1,300 ft in length per casting run.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OSU has mounted the membranes in spiral wound housings with a high packing density.  At the test at the National Carbon Capture centre the modules used were as follows:
texts.text: OSU has mounted the membranes in spiral wound housings with a high packing density.  At the test at the National Carbon Capture centre the modules used were as follows:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · 1 st - stage module (35m 2 ), 8-inch diameter x 24-inch long, 41x membrane leaves
texts.text: 1 st - stage module (35m 2 ), 8-inch diameter x 24-inch long, 41x membrane leaves

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · 2 nd - stage module (12m 2 ) - 5-inch diameter x 24-inch long
texts.text: 2 nd - stage module (12m 2 ) - 5-inch diameter x 24-inch long

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OSU refer to these spiral wound modules as 'commercial scale' using an increased membrane width.
texts.text: OSU refer to these spiral wound modules as 'commercial scale' using an increased membrane width.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The spriral wound membrane modules have a high packing density - OSU claim a packing density of 2,000m 2 /m 3 , which is backed up by the modules in the test.
texts.text: The spriral wound membrane modules have a high packing density - OSU claim a packing density of 2,000m 2 /m 3 , which is backed up by the modules in the test.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The modules have a very high pressure drop across at 1.5 psi/meter on each of the retentate and permeate sides.
texts.text: The modules have a very high pressure drop across at 1.5 psi/meter on each of the retentate and permeate sides.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The pre-treatment upstream of the membrane process comprised a Flue Gas Desulfurization (FGD) unit and a SO 2 Caustic Scrubber (SCS) unit which removed SO 2 to a very low level.  This and other literature suggest that SO 2 would damage the membrane, even at very low levels.  The gas is then humidified since the membrane requires water saturated gas for the facilitated transport mechanism to work.
texts.text: The pre-treatment upstream of the membrane process comprised a Flue Gas Desulfurization (FGD) unit and a SO 2 Caustic Scrubber (SCS) unit which removed SO 2 to a very low level.  This and other literature suggest that SO 2 would damage the membrane, even at very low levels.  The gas is then humidified since the membrane requires water saturated gas for the facilitated transport mechanism to work.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The membrane process itself is a standard 2-stage membrane process.  In the test at the National Carbon Capture Centre, the membranes were operated with a low differential pressure ratio of approximately 3.2 on the 1 st - stage and 4.4 on the 2 nd - stage.  This low differential pressure provides a relatively low driving force and is reflective of the typical operation of facilitated transport membranes.  Higher differential pressures do not necessarily increase the facilitated transport related flow, which can be limited by the availability of carrier sites, but would increase the solution diffusion related element, effectively decreasing the selectivity.
texts.text: The membrane process itself is a standard 2-stage membrane process.  In the test at the National Carbon Capture Centre, the membranes were operated with a low differential pressure ratio of approximately 3.2 on the 1 st - stage and 4.4 on the 2 nd - stage.  This low differential pressure provides a relatively low driving force and is reflective of the typical operation of facilitated transport membranes.  Higher differential pressures do not necessarily increase the facilitated transport related flow, which can be limited by the availability of carrier sites, but would increase the solution diffusion related element, effectively decreasing the selectivity.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: An expander was incorporated downstream of the 1 st -stage membrane to recover some of the energy of compression, thus decreasing the power consumption and the cost of capture.
texts.text: An expander was incorporated downstream of the 1 st -stage membrane to recover some of the energy of compression, thus decreasing the power consumption and the cost of capture.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In a relationship similar to that of CPT and Helmholtz/Hereon, Aqualung holds the licence to a patented membrane technology developed by the Norwegian University of Science and Technology (NTNU) in Trondheim, Norway.  The Aqualung team, however, seems to be smaller and less technical than the CPT team and seem to be more reliant on their investor group for technical expertise.  Aqualung chief executive Andrew Robbins said: 'Aqualung will lean on the entrepreneurial, engineering and scale up experience of our investor group to commercialise and roll out this technology ….'
texts.text: In a relationship similar to that of CPT and Helmholtz/Hereon, Aqualung holds the licence to a patented membrane technology developed by the Norwegian University of Science and Technology (NTNU) in Trondheim, Norway.  The Aqualung team, however, seems to be smaller and less technical than the CPT team and seem to be more reliant on their investor group for technical expertise.  Aqualung chief executive Andrew Robbins said: 'Aqualung will lean on the entrepreneurial, engineering and scale up experience of our investor group to commercialise and roll out this technology ….'

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The NTNU membrane being used by Aqualung is a hollow fibre composite membrane.  NTNU has developed a dip coating technique which can apply a thin (500 nm) amine coating to the external surface of a hollow fibre polymeric membrane supplied by a third party.
texts.text: The NTNU membrane being used by Aqualung is a hollow fibre composite membrane.  NTNU has developed a dip coating technique which can apply a thin (500 nm) amine coating to the external surface of a hollow fibre polymeric membrane supplied by a third party.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The performance figures being claimed by Aqualung or NTNU are not clear.  Both permeability and selectivity seem to be worse than those claimed by Ohio State University with permeability seeming to be significantly worse than PolyActive™.
texts.text: The performance figures being claimed by Aqualung or NTNU are not clear.  Both permeability and selectivity seem to be worse than those claimed by Ohio State University with permeability seeming to be significantly worse than PolyActive™.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It appears that the technology was initially licensed to Air Products prior to Aqualung.
texts.text: It appears that the technology was initially licensed to Air Products prior to Aqualung.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Aqualung claim a containerized solution (40-foot container) can capture between 15 and 50 ktpa of CO 2 from flue gases with concentrations ranging from 2% all the way to 30% and more (for the biogas sector). Current pilot rigs have sizes that range from 300 tpa to 4 ktpa.
texts.text: Aqualung claim a containerized solution (40-foot container) can capture between 15 and 50 ktpa of CO 2 from flue gases with concentrations ranging from 2% all the way to 30% and more (for the biogas sector). Current pilot rigs have sizes that range from 300 tpa to 4 ktpa.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The data in this report is taken from the results of that test reported in the International Journal of Greenhouse Gas Control 86 (2019) 191-200 'Field test of a pre-pilot scale hollow fibre facilitated transport membrane for CO 2 capture' (the 'Field Test' for the purposes of this document).
texts.text: The data in this report is taken from the results of that test reported in the International Journal of Greenhouse Gas Control 86 (2019) 191-200 'Field test of a pre-pilot scale hollow fibre facilitated transport membrane for CO 2 capture' (the 'Field Test' for the purposes of this document).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Aqualung's membrane was developed by NTNU.  It consists of a 500 nm selective layer, made of the Poly vinyl alcohol (PVA)/ amino acid salt hybrid, coated onto a hollow fibre polymer substrate provided by others (in the Field Test, a p-phenylene oxide (PPO) hollow fibre membrane was supplied by Parker, Norway;  Air Products provided membranes for earlier development and testing).
texts.text: Aqualung's membrane was developed by NTNU.  It consists of a 500 nm selective layer, made of the Poly vinyl alcohol (PVA)/ amino acid salt hybrid, coated onto a hollow fibre polymer substrate provided by others (in the Field Test, a p-phenylene oxide (PPO) hollow fibre membrane was supplied by Parker, Norway;  Air Products provided membranes for earlier development and testing).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It is not clear how Aqualung will commercially manufacture the membranes.  It would be reasonable to assume that they could follow a similar methodology and procure the base hollow fibre membranes from a third party and establish a facility to coat the membranes.  Similarly, it is not clear how Aqualung will manufacture the housings.  The membranes could be installed into conventional hollow fibre membrane housings so the manufacture could be outsourced.
texts.text: It is not clear how Aqualung will commercially manufacture the membranes.  It would be reasonable to assume that they could follow a similar methodology and procure the base hollow fibre membranes from a third party and establish a facility to coat the membranes.  Similarly, it is not clear how Aqualung will manufacture the housings.  The membranes could be installed into conventional hollow fibre membrane housings so the manufacture could be outsourced.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The membrane does not have a protective layer, meaning the active layer is directly exposed to the feed gas and any contaminants.
texts.text: The membrane does not have a protective layer, meaning the active layer is directly exposed to the feed gas and any contaminants.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The dip coating method used by NTNU to coat the membranes results in quite a thick selective layer, much thicker, for example, than the coating thicknesses achieved by OSU and Hereon.  It would be reasonable to assume, therefore, that the permeance of the membrane would be worse than OSU and Hereon and this seems to be the case with a permeance of up to 800 GPU being obtained in the laboratory and 650 GPU in the field.  A laboratory CO 2 /N 2 selectivity of 40 is quoted in the documents but this is very low for a facilitated transport membrane and might not have been measured at ideal conditions.
texts.text: The dip coating method used by NTNU to coat the membranes results in quite a thick selective layer, much thicker, for example, than the coating thicknesses achieved by OSU and Hereon.  It would be reasonable to assume, therefore, that the permeance of the membrane would be worse than OSU and Hereon and this seems to be the case with a permeance of up to 800 GPU being obtained in the laboratory and 650 GPU in the field.  A laboratory CO 2 /N 2 selectivity of 40 is quoted in the documents but this is very low for a facilitated transport membrane and might not have been measured at ideal conditions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In facilitated transport membranes, high GPU (SI equivalent units mol/m 2 .s.Pa) results can be obtained at low pressures, however, the actual flow across the membrane is determined by the transmission of CO 2 from carrier site to carrier site and by the availability of carrier sites.  Carrier saturation can occur when there are no free carrier molecules available to bond with carbon dioxide molecules.  This means that the actual flow of CO 2 across the membrane can reach a maximum irrespective of the differential pressure across the membrane (i.e. the apparent GPU will decrease significantly with increasing pressure).  This phenomenon was recognised in the Field Test report. Thus, while a high headline GPU suggests a low membrane area, in reality the permeance will be much lower and large membrane areas will be required.
texts.text: In facilitated transport membranes, high GPU (SI equivalent units mol/m 2 .s.Pa) results can be obtained at low pressures, however, the actual flow across the membrane is determined by the transmission of CO 2 from carrier site to carrier site and by the availability of carrier sites.  Carrier saturation can occur when there are no free carrier molecules available to bond with carbon dioxide molecules.  This means that the actual flow of CO 2 across the membrane can reach a maximum irrespective of the differential pressure across the membrane (i.e. the apparent GPU will decrease significantly with increasing pressure).  This phenomenon was recognised in the Field Test report. Thus, while a high headline GPU suggests a low membrane area, in reality the permeance will be much lower and large membrane areas will be required.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Facilitated transport membranes are susceptible to damage from contaminants, which can react with and deactivate the selective layer.  In the Field Test significant degradation of the membrane was observed over only a matter of days.  This degradation was clearly visible as a colouration of the membrane fibres.  This degradation was attributed to oxidation of the reactive carrier sites by the O 2 in the feed gas at the operating temperature.  In addition, the deposition of acidic water onto the membranes was also recognised as a potential cause of damage.
texts.text: Facilitated transport membranes are susceptible to damage from contaminants, which can react with and deactivate the selective layer.  In the Field Test significant degradation of the membrane was observed over only a matter of days.  This degradation was clearly visible as a colouration of the membrane fibres.  This degradation was attributed to oxidation of the reactive carrier sites by the O 2 in the feed gas at the operating temperature.  In addition, the deposition of acidic water onto the membranes was also recognised as a potential cause of damage.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The membranes must be operated over a narrow range of temperatures with tightly controlled humidity (the feed gas needs to be water saturated for the facilitated transport mechanism to work). In the Field Test an operating temperature of 90˚C was used.  At 80˚C condensation occurred, preventing the membrane from working, and above 90˚C the performance of the membrane rapidly declined and did not work at all at 115˚C.  This effect was due to a number of effects including a reduction in the humidity.
texts.text: The membranes must be operated over a narrow range of temperatures with tightly controlled humidity (the feed gas needs to be water saturated for the facilitated transport mechanism to work). In the Field Test an operating temperature of 90˚C was used.  At 80˚C condensation occurred, preventing the membrane from working, and above 90˚C the performance of the membrane rapidly declined and did not work at all at 115˚C.  This effect was due to a number of effects including a reduction in the humidity.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The effect of varying feed and permeate pressures was examined in the Field Test.  Operating with a higher feed pressure did not give rise to a linearly increase in flow across the membrane i.e. permeance decreases with increasing feed pressure.  This was recognised as being a feature of facilitated transport membranes, probably due to the effects of carrier saturation.  The application of a vacuum on the permeate side did have a positive increase in flow across the membrane but again the effects were limited to a narrow range of vacuum pressures.
texts.text: The effect of varying feed and permeate pressures was examined in the Field Test.  Operating with a higher feed pressure did not give rise to a linearly increase in flow across the membrane i.e. permeance decreases with increasing feed pressure.  This was recognised as being a feature of facilitated transport membranes, probably due to the effects of carrier saturation.  The application of a vacuum on the permeate side did have a positive increase in flow across the membrane but again the effects were limited to a narrow range of vacuum pressures.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The high headline selectivity and permeance figures achieved in the laboratory and in the small pilot test require very specific humidity and temperature operating conditions.  Away from the ideal conditions the performance of the membrane drops away very quickly, or the membrane will not work at all.
texts.text: The high headline selectivity and permeance figures achieved in the laboratory and in the small pilot test require very specific humidity and temperature operating conditions.  Away from the ideal conditions the performance of the membrane drops away very quickly, or the membrane will not work at all.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The ideal pressure/temperature/humidity conditions will be exceptionally difficult to achieve across a large membrane area and will require multiple intermediate, energy-intensive gas conditioning stages (heating and re-pressurisation) throughout the membrane area.  CAPEX is likely to be very expensive and energy use far more intensive than with CPT's PolyActive™ membrane.
texts.text: The ideal pressure/temperature/humidity conditions will be exceptionally difficult to achieve across a large membrane area and will require multiple intermediate, energy-intensive gas conditioning stages (heating and re-pressurisation) throughout the membrane area.  CAPEX is likely to be very expensive and energy use far more intensive than with CPT's PolyActive™ membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The susceptibility of the membrane to contaminants and damage from condensed water is also a real concern as was demonstrated by the rapid membrane degradation in the Field Test.
texts.text: The susceptibility of the membrane to contaminants and damage from condensed water is also a real concern as was demonstrated by the rapid membrane degradation in the Field Test.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The performance achieve appears to be similar, if not worse, than CPT's PolyActive™ based process.
texts.text: The performance achieve appears to be similar, if not worse, than CPT's PolyActive™ based process.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The advantage with facilitated transport membranes is their higher headline selectivity.  The NTNU membrane does not seem to have superior properties to PolyActive™.  The Field Test demonstrated the difficulty realising the benefits of facilitated transport membranes, even on a small scale.  The challenges associated with managing the temperature/pressure/humidity conditions were very obvious in the reported test results.  Achieving the optimum membrane performance in large-scale real-world applications is likely to be extremely challenging and at a significantly increased CAPEX over CPT's process.
texts.text: The advantage with facilitated transport membranes is their higher headline selectivity.  The NTNU membrane does not seem to have superior properties to PolyActive™.  The Field Test demonstrated the difficulty realising the benefits of facilitated transport membranes, even on a small scale.  The challenges associated with managing the temperature/pressure/humidity conditions were very obvious in the reported test results.  Achieving the optimum membrane performance in large-scale real-world applications is likely to be extremely challenging and at a significantly increased CAPEX over CPT's process.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The 'in the field' experience appears to be worse than CPT's PolyActive™ membrane.  In Field Test with a feed CO 2 concentration of 11.5-14.0%, NTNU achieved a maximum purity of 50% CO 2 downstream of the first stage.  In similar conditions, CPT achieves a purity of 50-60% (or higher) downstream of the first stage membrane.
texts.text: The 'in the field' experience appears to be worse than CPT's PolyActive™ membrane.  In Field Test with a feed CO 2 concentration of 11.5-14.0%, NTNU achieved a maximum purity of 50% CO 2 downstream of the first stage.  In similar conditions, CPT achieves a purity of 50-60% (or higher) downstream of the first stage membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPT's PolyActive™ membrane has a protective layer, which serves to protect the selective layer and to fill any manufacturing gaps in the very thin protective layer.  NTNU's membrane has no such protective layer which means the membrane will be more easily damaged during operation.  The selective coating on NTNU membrane is relatively thick so should be less susceptible to manufacturing defects.
texts.text: CPT's PolyActive™ membrane has a protective layer, which serves to protect the selective layer and to fill any manufacturing gaps in the very thin protective layer.  NTNU's membrane has no such protective layer which means the membrane will be more easily damaged during operation.  The selective coating on NTNU membrane is relatively thick so should be less susceptible to manufacturing defects.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Aqualung report a number of pilot tests but there is very little information in the public domain to allow these to be evaluated.
texts.text: Aqualung report a number of pilot tests but there is very little information in the public domain to allow these to be evaluated.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Field Test demonstrated the challenges in operating facilitated transport membranes in the field. Aqualung is more likely to encounter problems in further scaling due to the specific operating requirements of their facilitated transport membrane.  Contaminants, particularly SO 2 , need to be removed from the feed gas to very low levels.  Additionally, the feed gas needs to be water saturated for the facilitated transport mechanism to work.  Meeting this requirement whilst avoiding water condensing in the membrane housings will be extremely difficult to achieve across the large membrane areas required for large-scale carbon capture.  CPT's PolyActive™ membrane is tolerant of contaminants and can work over a wide range of operating conditions.  Avoiding water condensation is, therefore, relatively straight forward and major scaling problems in scaling are not anticipated.
texts.text: The Field Test demonstrated the challenges in operating facilitated transport membranes in the field. Aqualung is more likely to encounter problems in further scaling due to the specific operating requirements of their facilitated transport membrane.  Contaminants, particularly SO 2 , need to be removed from the feed gas to very low levels.  Additionally, the feed gas needs to be water saturated for the facilitated transport mechanism to work.  Meeting this requirement whilst avoiding water condensing in the membrane housings will be extremely difficult to achieve across the large membrane areas required for large-scale carbon capture.  CPT's PolyActive™ membrane is tolerant of contaminants and can work over a wide range of operating conditions.  Avoiding water condensation is, therefore, relatively straight forward and major scaling problems in scaling are not anticipated.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Aqualung use hollow fibre membranes in hollow fibre membrane housings.  The advantage of hollow fibre membranes is that they have a large surface area-to-volume ratio (i.e. a large membrane area can be packaged into a housing).  Housings can be operated in counter current mode for maximum efficiency.  Housings are a known technology and are low cost.
texts.text: Aqualung use hollow fibre membranes in hollow fibre membrane housings.  The advantage of hollow fibre membranes is that they have a large surface area-to-volume ratio (i.e. a large membrane area can be packaged into a housing).  Housings can be operated in counter current mode for maximum efficiency.  Housings are a known technology and are low cost.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The disadvantages of the hollow fibre housing is a higher pressure drop across the module.  The flow dynamics around the outside of the membrane fibres is more irregular so the membrane is potentially used less efficiently than a flat sheet module.  This means that additional membrane area will be required.  Many hundreds of housings will be required for a typical large capture plant.
texts.text: The disadvantages of the hollow fibre housing is a higher pressure drop across the module.  The flow dynamics around the outside of the membrane fibres is more irregular so the membrane is potentially used less efficiently than a flat sheet module.  This means that additional membrane area will be required.  Many hundreds of housings will be required for a typical large capture plant.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This pressure drop in the housings equates to energy loss which will have a very significant impact on the energy (kWh/tonne of CO 2 captured) required for capture.  CPT's housings have been designed to have extremely low-pressure losses (in the region of 1 psi/10,000m 2 ).
texts.text: This pressure drop in the housings equates to energy loss which will have a very significant impact on the energy (kWh/tonne of CO 2 captured) required for capture.  CPT's housings have been designed to have extremely low-pressure losses (in the region of 1 psi/10,000m 2 ).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Large temperature losses can be expected across Aqualung's membrane area in part due to the large pressure loss and in part due to environmental losses in the housings and interconnecting pipework. This temperature loss will exacerbate the challenge of maintaining water saturation throughout the membrane whilst avoiding condensation.  Lower temperatures in downstream membrane modules will have a significant impact on membrane permeance, which is markedly reduced (or is zero) at lower temperatures, as seen in the Field Test.  CPT's housing is inherently insulated and will not suffer the same temperature impact from either pressure or environmental losses.
texts.text: Large temperature losses can be expected across Aqualung's membrane area in part due to the large pressure loss and in part due to environmental losses in the housings and interconnecting pipework. This temperature loss will exacerbate the challenge of maintaining water saturation throughout the membrane whilst avoiding condensation.  Lower temperatures in downstream membrane modules will have a significant impact on membrane permeance, which is markedly reduced (or is zero) at lower temperatures, as seen in the Field Test.  CPT's housing is inherently insulated and will not suffer the same temperature impact from either pressure or environmental losses.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Consequently, the design of plants around Aqualung's membrane can be expected to be significantly more challenging with operating conditions varying significantly across the membrane area.  Plants will, therefore, be complex and susceptible to operational problems possibly resulting in damage to the membranes and more frequent change-out.  Achieving high levels of reliability can be expected to be challenging.  Plants designed around PolyActive™ are, in comparison, simple.  Operational problems are less likely to be encountered and high reliability and membrane life can be expected.
texts.text: Consequently, the design of plants around Aqualung's membrane can be expected to be significantly more challenging with operating conditions varying significantly across the membrane area.  Plants will, therefore, be complex and susceptible to operational problems possibly resulting in damage to the membranes and more frequent change-out.  Achieving high levels of reliability can be expected to be challenging.  Plants designed around PolyActive™ are, in comparison, simple.  Operational problems are less likely to be encountered and high reliability and membrane life can be expected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Despite the high permeance quoted, for the reasons given above, actual membrane areas are likely to be much larger using Aqualung's process than using CPT's process.  In addition, CPT's process can be operated at a higher pressure.
texts.text: Despite the high permeance quoted, for the reasons given above, actual membrane areas are likely to be much larger using Aqualung's process than using CPT's process.  In addition, CPT's process can be operated at a higher pressure.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It is believed that the process complexity will make the CAPEX of plants using Aqualung's membrane significantly more expensive than CPT's plants.  The high-pressure loss in the system will make the system much more energy intensive than CPT's plant.  The resulting cost of capture can be expected to be significantly higher.
texts.text: It is believed that the process complexity will make the CAPEX of plants using Aqualung's membrane significantly more expensive than CPT's plants.  The high-pressure loss in the system will make the system much more energy intensive than CPT's plant.  The resulting cost of capture can be expected to be significantly higher.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Literature from Darcy Partners, one of Aqualungs investors, says the company has pilots with Natural Gas Boilers (with Standard Lithium in Arkansas, USA), Lime Kilns (with SigmaRoc in Sweden), and Waste-to-Energy (with an Austrian PC).  Other projects in the pipeline include a steel facility and a refinery in March 2024.  The latter will be a very small unit of 300 tpa as a proof-of-concept for small scale applications.
texts.text: Literature from Darcy Partners, one of Aqualungs investors, says the company has pilots with Natural Gas Boilers (with Standard Lithium in Arkansas, USA), Lime Kilns (with SigmaRoc in Sweden), and Waste-to-Energy (with an Austrian PC).  Other projects in the pipeline include a steel facility and a refinery in March 2024.  The latter will be a very small unit of 300 tpa as a proof-of-concept for small scale applications.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Prior to Aqualung commercialising the technology, a test reported in the International Journal of Greenhouse Gas Control 86 (2019) 191-200 'Field test of a pre-pilot scale hollow fibre facilitated transport membrane for CO 2 capture' (the 'Field Test' for the purposes of this document).
texts.text: Prior to Aqualung commercialising the technology, a test reported in the International Journal of Greenhouse Gas Control 86 (2019) 191-200 'Field test of a pre-pilot scale hollow fibre facilitated transport membrane for CO 2 capture' (the 'Field Test' for the purposes of this document).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Field Test was carried out at Colacem Cement Plant located in Gubbio (PG) Italy.  The flue gas was drawn from gas from a sampling point in the flue, located 30 m below the top of the stack.  A vacuum pump was used to suck the flue gas through a 2 μm ceramic filter, which was used to remove the suspended particulate matter in the flue gas.  The temperature of the flue gas from the stack, during the plant tests, was about 115 °C.  The flue gas composition is given in the table below.
texts.text: The Field Test was carried out at Colacem Cement Plant located in Gubbio (PG) Italy.  The flue gas was drawn from gas from a sampling point in the flue, located 30 m below the top of the stack.  A vacuum pump was used to suck the flue gas through a 2 μm ceramic filter, which was used to remove the suspended particulate matter in the flue gas.  The temperature of the flue gas from the stack, during the plant tests, was about 115 °C.  The flue gas composition is given in the table below.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The membrane was characterised before the test with a CO 2 permeance of approximately 800 GPU and a selectivity of 40, both of which are extremely low for a facilitated transport membrane.
texts.text: The membrane was characterised before the test with a CO 2 permeance of approximately 800 GPU and a selectivity of 40, both of which are extremely low for a facilitated transport membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A membrane pump was used to suck the flue gas from the chimney. The basic operation conditions were feed flow 3.5 L/min; feed pressure 1.5 bar; temperature 90°C; no sweep gas.  This mean the flue gas was water saturated at 90˚C.
texts.text: A membrane pump was used to suck the flue gas from the chimney. The basic operation conditions were feed flow 3.5 L/min; feed pressure 1.5 bar; temperature 90°C; no sweep gas.  This mean the flue gas was water saturated at 90˚C.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The flue gas would have been water saturated at 90˚C.  When the temperature was lower than 80°C, the water vapor in the feed gas severely condensed in the tube and no reliable permeation data was obtained.  The optimum temperature was 90 °C, above which performance rapidly decreased with increasing temperature with no permeate flow above 115˚C.
texts.text: The flue gas would have been water saturated at 90˚C.  When the temperature was lower than 80°C, the water vapor in the feed gas severely condensed in the tube and no reliable permeation data was obtained.  The optimum temperature was 90 °C, above which performance rapidly decreased with increasing temperature with no permeate flow above 115˚C.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The results show that there is a narrow operating temperature range in facilitated transport membranes and a corresponding careful balance of temperature and humidity.  At lower temperatures water will condense in the membrane housing.  As temperature increases, gas diffusion increases and the rate of reaction between CO 2 and the carrier increases, resulting in an overall increase in CO 2 diffusion.  However, with increasing temperature, the sorption of CO 2 in the membrane decreases. Increasing temperature causes a decrement of water retention in the membrane due to the lower relative humidity, which in turn hinders the reaction and diffusion.
texts.text: The results show that there is a narrow operating temperature range in facilitated transport membranes and a corresponding careful balance of temperature and humidity.  At lower temperatures water will condense in the membrane housing.  As temperature increases, gas diffusion increases and the rate of reaction between CO 2 and the carrier increases, resulting in an overall increase in CO 2 diffusion.  However, with increasing temperature, the sorption of CO 2 in the membrane decreases. Increasing temperature causes a decrement of water retention in the membrane due to the lower relative humidity, which in turn hinders the reaction and diffusion.

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Field Test Impact of Temperature Variations
texts.text: Field Test Impact of Temperature Variations

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The feed pressure was varied in the range of 1.5 - 2.0 bar with no sweep gas or vacuum used on the permeate side.  The CO 2 flux across the membrane was seen to increase with feed pressure but the value is lower than the ideal case of solution-diffusion in a polymeric membrane (the red line in the graph below).  The report notes that this is probably due to the carrier-saturation phenomenon in facilitated transport membranes.  The report quotes the OSU reported a notable reduction of CO 2 permeability from ~3,000 Barrer to ~300 Barrer with a feed pressure increment from 2 bar to 28 bar, with a corresponding decrease in CO 2 /N 2 selectivity.
texts.text: The feed pressure was varied in the range of 1.5 - 2.0 bar with no sweep gas or vacuum used on the permeate side.  The CO 2 flux across the membrane was seen to increase with feed pressure but the value is lower than the ideal case of solution-diffusion in a polymeric membrane (the red line in the graph below).  The report notes that this is probably due to the carrier-saturation phenomenon in facilitated transport membranes.  The report quotes the OSU reported a notable reduction of CO 2 permeability from ~3,000 Barrer to ~300 Barrer with a feed pressure increment from 2 bar to 28 bar, with a corresponding decrease in CO 2 /N 2 selectivity.

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Field Test Impact of Feed Pressure on Flux
texts.text: Field Test Impact of Feed Pressure on Flux

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The effect of a vacuum on the permeate side was investigated in the Field Test and observed that an increase of CO 2 flux through the membrane can be observed when the vacuum was applied.  The CO 2 flux increased significantly with a vacuum pressure of 1.0 bar to 0.4 bar, however, when the vacuum grade was further reduced (at 0.2 bar), there was a slight reduction in CO 2 flux.  It was noted that high vacuum grade removes the water from the membrane so even with an increased driving force, the facilitated transport mechanism was hindered, leading to an overall reduction in the CO 2 flux across the membrane.
texts.text: The effect of a vacuum on the permeate side was investigated in the Field Test and observed that an increase of CO 2 flux through the membrane can be observed when the vacuum was applied.  The CO 2 flux increased significantly with a vacuum pressure of 1.0 bar to 0.4 bar, however, when the vacuum grade was further reduced (at 0.2 bar), there was a slight reduction in CO 2 flux.  It was noted that high vacuum grade removes the water from the membrane so even with an increased driving force, the facilitated transport mechanism was hindered, leading to an overall reduction in the CO 2 flux across the membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: NTNU note that it has been reported for many facilitated transport membranes that impurities in the feed gas could significantly hinder the facilitated transport effect, even if at small quantities.  SO x in particular was noted as a key component that could result in a significant reduction in CO 2 permeance.  In addition, oxidation of the facilitated transport agents was also noted as an issue if O 2 molecules were present under high temperature condition.  The carriers (e.g., amine group) are more likely to be oxidized overtime and the membrane will gradually lose its facilitated transport ability.
texts.text: NTNU note that it has been reported for many facilitated transport membranes that impurities in the feed gas could significantly hinder the facilitated transport effect, even if at small quantities.  SO x in particular was noted as a key component that could result in a significant reduction in CO 2 permeance.  In addition, oxidation of the facilitated transport agents was also noted as an issue if O 2 molecules were present under high temperature condition.  The carriers (e.g., amine group) are more likely to be oxidized overtime and the membrane will gradually lose its facilitated transport ability.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The stability of the facilitated transport membrane was monitored for a duration of one week, and a reduction of CO 2 flux was recorded.  The effect of the suspended particulate matter or the so-called 'fly ash fouling', the oxidation of the facilitated transport agents under the relatively high temperature condition with the presence of O 2 , and the effect of the acidic components in the feed stream may be the main causes of the reduced separation performance.
texts.text: The stability of the facilitated transport membrane was monitored for a duration of one week, and a reduction of CO 2 flux was recorded.  The effect of the suspended particulate matter or the so-called 'fly ash fouling', the oxidation of the facilitated transport agents under the relatively high temperature condition with the presence of O 2 , and the effect of the acidic components in the feed stream may be the main causes of the reduced separation performance.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The loss of membrane performance in the Field Test was believed to be due to the oxidation of the reactive carrier sites by the O 2 in the feed gas at the operating temperature.  In addition, the occurrence of acidic water on the membranes may generate potential damage to the material.
texts.text: The loss of membrane performance in the Field Test was believed to be due to the oxidation of the reactive carrier sites by the O 2 in the feed gas at the operating temperature.  In addition, the occurrence of acidic water on the membranes may generate potential damage to the material.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A colour change was observed: the fresh membrane shows a white colour (top in the image below), while after the field test, the membrane becomes brownish in colour (bottom).
texts.text: A colour change was observed: the fresh membrane shows a white colour (top in the image below), while after the field test, the membrane becomes brownish in colour (bottom).

texts.orig: Field Test Membrane (before top and after below)
