texts.label: text
texts.orig: Aqualung's membrane consists of a 500 nm selective layer, made of the Poly vinyl alcohol (PVA)/ amino acid salt hybrid, coated onto a hollow fibre polymer substrate provided by others (in the Field Test, a p-phenylene oxide (PPO) hollow fibre membrane was supplied by Parker, Norway;  Air Products provided membranes for earlier development and testing).
texts.text: Aqualung's membrane consists of a 500 nm selective layer, made of the Poly vinyl alcohol (PVA)/ amino acid salt hybrid, coated onto a hollow fibre polymer substrate provided by others (in the Field Test, a p-phenylene oxide (PPO) hollow fibre membrane was supplied by Parker, Norway;  Air Products provided membranes for earlier development and testing).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The coating procedure is given as follows. The PPO hollow fibres were first washed to remove surface dust.  They were then dipped into the coating solution for 10 s, vertically withdrawn, and hung in the air for about 6 h before the second coating.  The coated membranes were turned up-side-down and dip-coated again following the same procedure to eliminate defects, and the membranes were hung in the air for another 6 h after the second coating.  Finally, the coated fibres were dried in a vacuum oven at 60 °C for at least 6 h before further characterization.
texts.text: The coating procedure is given as follows. The PPO hollow fibres were first washed to remove surface dust.  They were then dipped into the coating solution for 10 s, vertically withdrawn, and hung in the air for about 6 h before the second coating.  The coated membranes were turned up-side-down and dip-coated again following the same procedure to eliminate defects, and the membranes were hung in the air for another 6 h after the second coating.  Finally, the coated fibres were dried in a vacuum oven at 60 °C for at least 6 h before further characterization.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It should be noted that the membrane has no protective layer so any damage to the membrane will directly affect the selective layer.
texts.text: It should be noted that the membrane has no protective layer so any damage to the membrane will directly affect the selective layer.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It is not clear how Aqualung will commercially manufacture the membranes or the manufacturing experience that Aqualung has.  It would be reasonable to assume that they could follow a similar methodology as the Field Test and procure the base hollow fibre membranes from a third party and establish a facility to coat the membranes.
texts.text: It is not clear how Aqualung will commercially manufacture the membranes or the manufacturing experience that Aqualung has.  It would be reasonable to assume that they could follow a similar methodology as the Field Test and procure the base hollow fibre membranes from a third party and establish a facility to coat the membranes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Similarly, it is not clear how Aqualung will manufacture the housings.  The membranes could be installed into conventional hollow fibre membrane housings so the manufacture could be outsourced.
texts.text: Similarly, it is not clear how Aqualung will manufacture the housings.  The membranes could be installed into conventional hollow fibre membrane housings so the manufacture could be outsourced.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For the Field Test, the hollow fibre membrane module was fabricated by putting 70 hollow fibres in a inch stainless tube sealed with Epoxy glue giving a membrane area of around 200 cm 2 .
texts.text: For the Field Test, the hollow fibre membrane module was fabricated by putting 70 hollow fibres in a inch stainless tube sealed with Epoxy glue giving a membrane area of around 200 cm 2 .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Compact Membrane Systems (CMS) was originally spun off from DuPont in 1993.  CMS develops fluoropolymer membranes for difficult separations in industrial gas and liquid applications.  Since 2011 CMS has focused its efforts on the development of its Optiperm™ membrane, a technology to allow modular separation of olefins and paraffins.  Optiperm™ uses a facilitated transport mechanism to pass olefins across a membrane while leaving the paraffins behind, in contrast to the incumbent distillation technology.  In the last couple of years CMS has made a shift from a 20+ year membrane research organisation into a commercially driven company with carbon capture an increasing area of focus.
texts.text: Compact Membrane Systems (CMS) was originally spun off from DuPont in 1993.  CMS develops fluoropolymer membranes for difficult separations in industrial gas and liquid applications.  Since 2011 CMS has focused its efforts on the development of its Optiperm™ membrane, a technology to allow modular separation of olefins and paraffins.  Optiperm™ uses a facilitated transport mechanism to pass olefins across a membrane while leaving the paraffins behind, in contrast to the incumbent distillation technology.  In the last couple of years CMS has made a shift from a 20+ year membrane research organisation into a commercially driven company with carbon capture an increasing area of focus.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Very little information on Compact Membrane Systems' Optiperm™ carbon is available in the public domain.  The limited write-up here is based on a review of published press releases and a review of video/podcast information where CMS has participated.
texts.text: Very little information on Compact Membrane Systems' Optiperm™ carbon is available in the public domain.  The limited write-up here is based on a review of published press releases and a review of video/podcast information where CMS has participated.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CMS's Optiperm™ c arbon membrane is a fluoropolymer facilitated transport membrane.  It is believed that it is in hollow fibre form but contradictory information is available with some stating that it is in flat sheet format.
texts.text: CMS's Optiperm™ c arbon membrane is a fluoropolymer facilitated transport membrane.  It is believed that it is in hollow fibre form but contradictory information is available with some stating that it is in flat sheet format.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The EU (and other countries) are seeking to ban Perfluoroalkyl and Polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS).  Suppliers such as Solvay, 3M, and others are voluntarily phasing out the use of PFAS, which has resulted in shutting down the production of PTFE.  It is unclear how this ban could affect CMS's membrane but it is a significant potential risk in many jurisdictions.
texts.text: The EU (and other countries) are seeking to ban Perfluoroalkyl and Polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS).  Suppliers such as Solvay, 3M, and others are voluntarily phasing out the use of PFAS, which has resulted in shutting down the production of PTFE.  It is unclear how this ban could affect CMS's membrane but it is a significant potential risk in many jurisdictions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CMS claims their membrane has antifouling properties, although it is not clear how this is achieved (this is achieved in liquid applications by backwashing through the membrane i.e. reversing the flow). CMS claim initial lab results show a permeability of 4000 - 6000 GPU but no figures on selectivity were available.
texts.text: CMS claims their membrane has antifouling properties, although it is not clear how this is achieved (this is achieved in liquid applications by backwashing through the membrane i.e. reversing the flow). CMS claim initial lab results show a permeability of 4000 - 6000 GPU but no figures on selectivity were available.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The technology is currently at TRL5 and is being trialled at small pilot scale with a number of parties. CMS have recently announced projects with  RHI Magnesita, OMV and Voestalpine to start at the end of 2023 or start of 2024.  These are thought to be very small scale 'bench' type tests that are conducted at the premises of the announced companies but not necessarily connected to their facilities (the test rig at Voestalpine, for example, is part of a technology accelerator programme and is thought to use a synthetic test gas).  A larger 1-4 ton/day pilot has been announced with Braskem, to start at the end of 2024 or start of 2025.
texts.text: The technology is currently at TRL5 and is being trialled at small pilot scale with a number of parties. CMS have recently announced projects with  RHI Magnesita, OMV and Voestalpine to start at the end of 2023 or start of 2024.  These are thought to be very small scale 'bench' type tests that are conducted at the premises of the announced companies but not necessarily connected to their facilities (the test rig at Voestalpine, for example, is part of a technology accelerator programme and is thought to use a synthetic test gas).  A larger 1-4 ton/day pilot has been announced with Braskem, to start at the end of 2024 or start of 2025.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CMS claims to have made significant progress towards their goal of delivering a low-cost, fully electrified commercial carbon capture solution by 2026.  This seems highly optimistic given the current state of the technology.
texts.text: CMS claims to have made significant progress towards their goal of delivering a low-cost, fully electrified commercial carbon capture solution by 2026.  This seems highly optimistic given the current state of the technology.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It is extremely difficult to draw a comparison between CMS and CPT's technology due to the lack of public domain information on CMS' product.
texts.text: It is extremely difficult to draw a comparison between CMS and CPT's technology due to the lack of public domain information on CMS' product.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It seems that CMS are at least 2 years behind CPT and 2 levels of TRL behind CPT.
texts.text: It seems that CMS are at least 2 years behind CPT and 2 levels of TRL behind CPT.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There is no information to suggest that CMS' membrane will avoid the problems that are encountered by facilitated transport membranes.  CMS are claiming very high permeabilities, however, in common with other facilitated transport membranes, actual performance outside of the laboratory can be expected to be a small percentage of the claimed performance.
texts.text: There is no information to suggest that CMS' membrane will avoid the problems that are encountered by facilitated transport membranes.  CMS are claiming very high permeabilities, however, in common with other facilitated transport membranes, actual performance outside of the laboratory can be expected to be a small percentage of the claimed performance.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CMS's Optiperm™ c arbon membrane is a fluoropolymer facilitated transport membrane.  CMS claim it has antifouling properties, although it is not clear how this is achieved (this is achieved by backwashing, for example, in liquid applications).  CMS claim initial lab results show a permeability of 4000 - 6000 GPU but no figures on selectivity were available.
texts.text: CMS's Optiperm™ c arbon membrane is a fluoropolymer facilitated transport membrane.  CMS claim it has antifouling properties, although it is not clear how this is achieved (this is achieved by backwashing, for example, in liquid applications).  CMS claim initial lab results show a permeability of 4000 - 6000 GPU but no figures on selectivity were available.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It is believed that the membrane is in hollow fibre format but CMS have also released text that say 'Work is continuing to scale up the membrane on the same spiral wound platform as existing Optiperm™ products'.
texts.text: It is believed that the membrane is in hollow fibre format but CMS have also released text that say 'Work is continuing to scale up the membrane on the same spiral wound platform as existing Optiperm™ products'.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The EU (and other countries) are seeking to ban Perfluoroalkyl and Polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS).  Suppliers such as Solvay, 3M, and others are voluntarily phasing out the use of PFAS, which has resulted in shutting down the production of PTFE.  It is unclear how this ban could affect CMS's membrane but it is a potential risk.
texts.text: The EU (and other countries) are seeking to ban Perfluoroalkyl and Polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS).  Suppliers such as Solvay, 3M, and others are voluntarily phasing out the use of PFAS, which has resulted in shutting down the production of PTFE.  It is unclear how this ban could affect CMS's membrane but it is a potential risk.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It is understood that NOx and SOx permeate the membrane.  CO does not pass through the membrane.
texts.text: It is understood that NOx and SOx permeate the membrane.  CO does not pass through the membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CMS claim the membrane will only need to be replaced every 6-8 years.  This seems to be grossly optimistic for a facilitated transport membrane.
texts.text: CMS claim the membrane will only need to be replaced every 6-8 years.  This seems to be grossly optimistic for a facilitated transport membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CMS's process is a 2-stage process with recycle very similar to other membrane processes.  The system appears to be operated with a positive feed pressure of 2.5 - 4.0 bar.  No information is given on whether a vacuum will be used on the permeate side of the membrane.
texts.text: CMS's process is a 2-stage process with recycle very similar to other membrane processes.  The system appears to be operated with a positive feed pressure of 2.5 - 4.0 bar.  No information is given on whether a vacuum will be used on the permeate side of the membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: No information is given on CMS' ability to manufacture membranes at commercial scale.
texts.text: No information is given on CMS' ability to manufacture membranes at commercial scale.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: No information is given on CMS' housing technology.  It appears that the membranes will be produced in hollow fibre of flat sheet spiral wound form.
texts.text: No information is given on CMS' housing technology.  It appears that the membranes will be produced in hollow fibre of flat sheet spiral wound form.
